data.title: GSR Launches First Multi-Asset Crypto ETF on Nasdaq
data.description:
Cryptocurrency market maker GSR has introduced its first multi-asset crypto ETF, named GSR Crypto Core3 ETF, with the ticker BESO, now listed on Nasdaq. According to ChainCatcher, the ETF includes investments in Bitcoin (BTC), Ethereum (ETH), and Solana (SOL), and incorporates staking yields where applicable.

The fund employs an active management strategy with plans to adjust its holdings weekly, and carries a management fee of 1%. The report highlights that this product is the first actively managed multi-asset crypto ETF in the United States to offer staking capabilities. This launch marks GSR's latest effort to expand its crypto ETF and asset management business.

data.title: WalletConnect Expands to Canton Network, Enhancing Institutional Finance Access
data.description: WalletConnect has integrated with the Canton Network, expanding its ecosystem of 55.5 million users to include access to the institutional finance blockchain. According to NS3.AI, this integration connects over 700 wallets and 70,000 decentralized applications, focusing on stablecoin payments, tokenized real-world assets, and decentralized finance (DeFi) use cases. Canton Network reportedly processes over $8 trillion in tokenized assets each month and settles more than $350 billion in U.S. Treasuries daily.

data.title: Polygon Labs Head Discusses Bridge Operations Post-KelpDAO Exploit
data.description: Polygon Labs Head of Product John Egan highlighted the resilience of Agglayer's proof-based bridge design following the KelpDAO exploit, which resulted in the draining of $292 million of rsETH. According to NS3.AI, Egan noted that despite the hack, Polygon PoS and Agglayer bridges continued to process approximately $200 million. Meanwhile, Aave experienced significant activity with $6.6 billion in withdrawals, and several protocols opted to pause related markets or bridges in response to the incident.

data.title: Sources: Trump is willing to give Iran another 3 to 5 days of ceasefire.
data.description: Axios, a US news website, reported on the 22nd, citing a US source, that US President Trump was willing to give Iran another 3 to 5 days of ceasefire, but "this will not be indefinite." (Xinhua)

data.title: European Commission Introduces New Energy Package Ahead of Cyprus Summit
data.description: The European Commission has introduced a new energy package as European Union leaders prepare for a summit in Cyprus scheduled for Thursday and Friday. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the significance of this development in the context of ongoing energy challenges faced by the EU. The package aims to address energy security and sustainability, focusing on reducing dependency on external energy sources and promoting renewable energy initiatives. This move comes amid heightened concerns over energy supply disruptions and the need for a cohesive strategy to tackle climate change. The summit in Cyprus will provide a platform for EU leaders to discuss and potentially endorse the proposed measures, which are seen as crucial for the bloc's energy future.

data.title: Documentary Searches for Satoshi Nakamoto: Hal Finney and Len Sassaman May Be Co-Creators of Bitcoin
data.description: The documentary *Finding Satoshi*, released Wednesday, claims that Bitcoin creator Satoshi Nakamoto is not a single person, but an alias used by cryptographers Hal Finney and Len Sassaman. Directors Tucker Tooley and Matthew Miele, through a four-year investigation, indicate that Hal Finney was responsible for writing the Bitcoin code, while Len Sassaman was responsible for writing the written content, including the white paper. Hal Finney's widow, Fran Finney, admitted in an interview that her husband may have been involved in the creation of Bitcoin. Investigators used elimination methods to rule out other candidates, such as Adam Back, noting that Hal Finney and Len Sassaman's online activity records closely matched those of Satoshi Nakamoto. Additionally, the film included a 90-minute interview with Sam Bankman-Fried, but it was ultimately not used.

data.title: Kelp DAO Loses $292 Million in Hack Linked to North Korea's Lazarus Group
data.description:
Kelp DAO suffered a significant loss of $292 million due to a hack on Saturday. According to NS3.AI, preliminary findings by LayerZero suggest that the attack was carried out by TraderTraitor, a subgroup of North Korea's Lazarus Group. Kelp DAO attributed the exploit to a failure in LayerZero's infrastructure. LayerZero explained that Kelp DAO had used a single verifier configuration to approve cross-chain messages, which may have contributed to the vulnerability.

This incident, combined with the $285 million Drift exploit, has brought the total suspected North Korea-linked crypto theft to at least $578 million across major incidents in April.

data.title: Iranian Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Condemns Ongoing Maritime Blockade
data.description: Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesperson has criticized the continued maritime blockade on Iranian ports, labeling it as an aggressive action. According to Odaily, the spokesperson emphasized the negative impact of the blockade on Iran's economy and international trade. The blockade has been a point of contention, affecting the country's ability to engage in global commerce effectively.

data.title: Majority of Economists Predict Federal Reserve Rate Cut This Year
data.description: A survey conducted by Reuters reveals that 71 out of 103 economists anticipate at least one interest rate cut by the Federal Reserve this year. According to ChainCatcher, this expectation reflects growing sentiment among economists regarding potential monetary policy adjustments by the U.S. central bank.

data.title: Sullivan & Cromwell Revises Filing in Prince Group Case Due to AI Errors
data.description: Sullivan & Cromwell informed a U.S. bankruptcy court that an April 9 filing in a Chapter 15 case associated with Prince Group contained errors generated by AI, including fictitious citations. According to NS3.AI, the firm withdrew the initial motion and submitted a revised version after lawyers representing Prince Group and Chen Zhi contested the cited authorities. Defendants claimed that at least 28 citations were incorrect, and the case involves claims over billions of dollars in cryptocurrency, which prosecutors allege is connected to scam activities.

data.title: Coinbase launches tGBP, a stablecoin denominated by British pounds.
data.description: According to an official announcement, Coinbase announced that it will launch the fiat-backed stablecoin tGBP starting April 22, 2026. Users can buy, sell, exchange, send, and receive tGBP through the Coinbase App and Coinbase Exchange.

data.title: Crypto Tax Rules Create Reporting Burden, Data Shows
data.description: The absence of a de minimis exemption for cryptocurrency payments and the taxation of staking rewards upon receipt are creating a significant reporting burden, according to CoinDesk. This situation complicates tax compliance for individuals and businesses involved in crypto transactions, as they must meticulously track and report even minor transactions. The data highlights the challenges faced by the crypto community under the current tax framework.

data.title: Kraken submitted 56 million crypto tax forms for the 2025 tax year, one-third of which involved transactions under one dollar.
data.description: Kraken has submitted 56 million crypto transaction forms to the U.S. IRS for the 2025 tax year. Of these, approximately 18.5 million forms involved transactions under $1, and more than half involved amounts under $10. Data shows that only 8.5% of 1099-DA forms exceeded $600, and 74% involved amounts under $50. Kraken stated that the lack of micro-tax exemptions means crypto payments and staking rewards incur tax liabilities upon receipt, increasing the reporting burden on investors. Kraken proposes that Congress pass a broad inflation-linked micro-tax exemption policy and allow taxpayers to choose when to pay their staking rewards.

data.title: Economists Predict Fed to Maintain Rates Until September
data.description: A recent Reuters survey reveals that out of 103 economists, 56 believe the U.S. Federal Reserve will keep the federal funds rate within the 3.50%-3.75% range until September. According to Jin10, this contrasts with a previous survey conducted in late March, where at least one of the 82 economists predicted an earlier rate cut.

data.title: U.S. PCE Inflation Forecasted to Decline Through 2026
data.description: According to Jin10, a Reuters survey indicates that U.S. Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) inflation is projected to average 3.7% in the second quarter, 3.4% in the third quarter, and 3.2% in the fourth quarter of 2026. These figures represent an upward revision from a late March survey, which forecasted PCE inflation at 3.3%, 3.1%, and 2.9% for the respective quarters. The survey reflects expectations of a gradual decline in inflation rates as the year progresses.

data.title: Mascots, Aliens, and the Eccentric 'Sock People'
data.description: A recent exploration into the world of mascots, aliens, and the quirky 'sock people' reveals a fascinating subculture. According to PANews, these unique characters have captured the imagination of many, blending creativity with a touch of eccentricity. The phenomenon has gained traction, with enthusiasts celebrating the whimsical nature of these figures. The 'sock people' are particularly intriguing, often crafted with vibrant colors and imaginative designs, showcasing the artistic flair of their creators. This cultural trend highlights the diverse ways individuals express themselves through unconventional mediums, offering a glimpse into the playful side of human creativity.

data.title: Swedish Central Bank Governor: Inflation and Energy Prices Unlikely to Ease Quickly Despite Potential Hormuz Strait Agreement
data.description: Swedish Central Bank Governor Stefan Ingves has expressed skepticism about the immediate impact of a potential agreement to open the Hormuz Strait on inflation and energy prices. According to Jin10, Ingves stated that even if a formal agreement is reached in the near term, the pressures from inflation and elevated energy costs will not be alleviated swiftly by such a deal. The Hormuz Strait is a critical chokepoint for global oil shipments, and any disruptions or agreements concerning its accessibility can have significant implications for energy markets worldwide. Ingves' comments highlight the complexities involved in addressing inflationary pressures and the multifaceted nature of energy price dynamics.

data.title: Swedish Central Bank Governor Teden Discusses Economic Outlook
data.description: Swedish Central Bank Governor Teden stated that the country's lower inflation starting point provides some leeway to better understand the economic consequences. According to Jin10, Teden emphasized the importance of assessing the economic impact with clarity, given the current inflationary conditions. This perspective allows for a more informed approach to monetary policy decisions.

data.title: Sweden's Central Bank Governor Warns of Rising Inflation Risks
data.description: Sweden's Central Bank Governor, Erik Thedéen, has expressed concerns about the increasing risk of inflation surpassing previous expectations. According to Jin10, Thedéen noted that the likelihood of inflation being higher than anticipated just a few weeks ago has grown. This statement highlights the ongoing challenges faced by central banks in managing inflationary pressures amid global economic uncertainties.

data.title: Anonymous Wallet Nets $1.17M from ASTEROID Token Trade
data.description: An anonymous wallet executed a highly profitable trade by purchasing 2.79 billion ASTEROID tokens for $575 on April 17 and selling them for 503 ETH, valued at $1.17 million, on Tuesday. This transaction is considered one of the year's cleanest memecoin trades, according to CoinDesk.
